owning_subsystem: schemas/surrealdb, schemas/crates/surrealdb-model, shared/surrealdb-access, shared/governed-storage, services/knowledge-service, workflows/knowledge_publication, platform/runtime, docs, and plans
non_goals: Do not move approval semantics, workflow sequencing, or cross-service orchestration into SurrealDB.
Do not add direct UI-to-SurrealDB access.
Do not migrate non-knowledge domains in this umbrella beyond reusable shared substrate support.
scope_in: Upgrade and unify the workspace SurrealDB dependency.
Add versioned SurrealDB DDL artifacts for schemafull knowledge-domain tables, relations, indexes, projections, and change propagation.
Add typed retrieval contracts and shared repository ports for searchable knowledge chunks, lineage traversals, publication read models, and typed change notifications.
Add durable shared SurrealDB access helpers including configuration, DDL bootstrapping, transactions, retrieval, and projection queries.
Migrate the data_knowledge runtime path from memory-provider retrieval to SurrealDB-backed storage and search.
Add execution artifacts, tests, and supporting documentation updates.
scope_out: New managed database infrastructure outside the existing host/runtime model.
UI product changes or direct client-side database connectivity.
Governance, finance, or other non-knowledge domain migrations beyond reusable substrate changes.
acceptance_criteria: The data_knowledge path uses SurrealDB-backed canonical storage, searchable retrieval, graph lineage, and materialized latest-publication-status views.
Runtime retrieval consumers use typed shared contracts and ports rather than memory-provider search.
Direct SurrealDB usage remains confined to shared/surrealdb-access.
Workflow-stage persistence for touched knowledge mutations is atomic and leaves no partial records, projections, or emitted change notifications on failure.
Schema registry tests cover the new DDL artifacts and retrieval/read-model contract surfaces.
validation_artifacts: Passing schema registry and contract tests for DDL and retrieval additions
rollback_path: Revert the SurrealDB version uplift, DDL artifacts, shared access-layer expansion, data_knowledge migration, tests, and documentation together so the repository returns coherently to the previous in-memory retrieval posture.
